arm: B — amortization / transfer of one harness-mine discovery across a parser family
mutator: missing-end-anchor ($/ -> /; accept trailing garbage), discovered+twice-verified on cron (../cron-capstone)

cron.note: parseInt-truncation of 5abc is subtle; permissive suite misses it

hexcolor.note: mutant accepts #1234567/#123456xx; permissive old-1 CATCHES it (passRate 0.963, 80 cases fail) — good-faith hex suite tests trailing garbage

ipv4.note: parseInt range-validation idiom; the regex-anchor mutator does not apply

verdict: NULL by idiosyncratic blind spot — the cron blind spot does NOT recur across the family. Good-faith suites for obvious-malformed parsers (hex) already catch it; ipv4 uses a different idiom. Baking the cron discovery once does not harden the family. Amortization of this discovery is cron-specific.
note: Mechanism (harness-mine discover+bake on cron) is unaffected and still works; this tests TRANSFER, which fails here.
